question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles - Toss Match Double Saint Helena Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if  Saint Helena is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

events.title: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles - Toss Match Double
events.description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if  Saint Helena is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B toss-match double between Saint Helena and Seychelles remains tightly clustered around 50-51%, underscoring evenly matched minnows with sparse head-to-head data— their first encounter. No official injuries or lineup changes reported, but both sides enter off recent qualifier losses (Saint Helena to Cameroon, Seychelles to Nigeria), fueling desperation for points in Entebbe Oval's bowler-friendly conditions. Toss looms large, as dew could favor chasing teams, while intermittent rain risks elevate draw pricing; historical T20 trends show 50/50 toss splits amplifying unpredictability in low-stakes qualifiers where upsets thrive on momentum swings.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles - Most Sixes Saint Helena Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Seychelles.The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Saint Helena.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash between Saint Helena and Seychelles, trader consensus splits evenly at 50% for Seychelles, Saint Helena, or a tie on most sixes, underscoring their near-identical limited batting profiles. Both minnow nations average under 2.5 sixes per innings in recent ICC qualifiers, with Seychelles edging Saint Helena 4-3 in combined sixes across four T20Is last year amid bowler-friendly pitches in Entebbe. No confirmed lineups or injury updates shift the balance, while flat conditions could spark a rare hitting duel, keeping probabilities bunched as traders weigh head-to-head parity and low-event variance typical of sub-regional play.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles - Team Top Batter Saint Helena Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Saint Helena.
The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Seychelles.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Tanzania
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Eswatini and Tanzania scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Tanzania sits atop Group B standings after emphatic wins over Seychelles (by 106 runs) and Ghana (by 8 wickets), boasting a potent batting lineup led by Abhik Patwa's explosive 81* and bowling spearheaded by Sanjay Kumar (4/10). question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Tanzania - Most Sixes Eswatini Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Eswatini and Tanzania scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if Eswatini is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Tanzania.The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if Tanzania is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Eswatini.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Eswatini vs Tanzania T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B most sixes market hovers near even—SWZ at 50.5%, TZA at 50.0%, draw at 49.5%—driven by balanced batting firepower from both sides in recent outings. Eswatini's middle order, led by aggressive hitters like Ghulan Mohammed, exploded for 12 sixes in their win over Cameroon, mirroring Tanzania's 14 sixes via Abhik Patwa and others against Nigeria. The Entebbe Oval pitch, known for short boundaries and true bounce, amplifies big-hitting parity, with no dominant bowler suppressing either lineup. Clear weather and full-strength squads keep upsets viable, sustaining the tight race amid T20's volatility.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Malawi vs Ghana
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Malawi and Ghana scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ghana holds a clear edge in trader consensus for the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash against Malawi, driven by their dominant 46-run victory over Malawi in their last meeting in December 2023, where Ghana posted 110/6 and bowled Malawi out for 64. Ghana's balanced attack, led by all-rounder Laweence Akyea and spinner Vincent Ateakayi, exploited Malawi's fragile top order, a persistent issue evident in recent losses to Nigeria and Rwanda. Malawi's pace bowler Usama Khan remains a threat, but their batting lacks depth amid inconsistent T20I form. Played on a spin-assisting pitch in Entebbe, Uganda, weather forecasts suggest minimal rain disruption, tilting implied probabilities toward Ghana amid both teams' push for Africa qualifier spots.

description:

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Malawi will be considered correct if  Malawi is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Malawi vs Ghana - Most Sixes Malawi Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Malawi and Ghana scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Malawi will be considered correct if Malawi is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Ghana.The outcome corresponding to Ghana will be considered correct if Ghana is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Malawi.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa: New Zealand vs South Africa
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand holds a strong edge in trader consensus for the opening T20I against South Africa, driven by their impeccable 8-0 home T20I record over the past two years and recent momentum from a 3-0 ODI sweep against Bangladesh. Wellington's seaming conditions favor NZ's seamers like Trent Boult, but SA's explosive hitters like Heinrich Klaasen could spark an upset if dew aids chasing; watch weather and toss for pivot points in this series opener.

description:

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if  New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Afghanistan's surging ODI form has emerged as the key driver of trader consensus favoring them in the Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka ODI series markets, with implied probabilities on Polymarket hovering around 55-60% for an Afghan series win or strong performance. The hosts recently swept Bangladesh 2-0 in July 2024, showcasing Rashid Khan's spin dominance (15 wickets in recent outings) and Ibrahim Zadran's batting consistency, while Sri Lanka's campaign faltered with a 0-3 whitewash by India amid injuries to Wanindu Hasaranga and Dushmantha Chameera. Historically, Sri Lanka leads head-to-head 12-4, but Afghanistan's home edge in UAE conditions—spin-friendly pitches—and superior current ICC ranking (7th vs 8th) tilt sentiment their way. Watch for lineup confirmations and weather, as rain-shortened games amplify upset potential.

This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-24 in ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Afghanistan will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Afghanistan.
The outcome corresponding to Sri Lanka will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Sri Lanka.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka ODI team top batter market remains deadlocked with draw odds leading at 50%, reflecting evenly matched batting attacks amid no major disruptions. Afghanistan's opener Ibrahim Zadran enters on a hot streak with back-to-back fifties in recent ODIs, backed by Rahmanullah Gurbaz's explosive starts, while Sri Lanka counters with captain Charith Asalanka's consistent middle-order anchoring and Kusal Mendis's venue form at Colombo. Official reports confirm full-strength lineups, no injuries, and a batsman-friendly pitch forecast favoring high totals where top scores often cluster closely. Head-to-head history shows tight run chases, amplifying draw appeal as wisdom-of-crowds pricing captures the fine margins in this opener.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Konark Suryas Odisha
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Konark Suryas Odisha scheduled for March 22 2026 in Legends Cricket League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Konark Suryas Odisha - Toss Match Double Mumbai Spartans Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Konark Suryas Odisha scheduled for 2026-03-22 in Legends Cricket League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Mumbai Spartans will be considered correct if  Mumbai Spartans is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Konark Suryas Odisha will be considered correct if Konark Suryas Odisha is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Konark Suryas Odisha - Most Sixes Mumbai Spartans Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Konark Suryas Odisha scheduled for 2026-03-22 in Legends Cricket League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Mumbai Spartans will be considered correct if Mumbai Spartans is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Konark Suryas Odisha.The outcome corresponding to Konark Suryas Odisha will be considered correct if Konark Suryas Odisha is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Mumbai Spartans.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Southern Super Stars
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Southern Super Stars scheduled for March 23 2026 in Legends Cricket League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Southern Super Stars - Toss Match Double Mumbai Spartans Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Southern Super Stars scheduled for 2026-03-23 in Legends Cricket League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Mumbai Spartans will be considered correct if  Mumbai Spartans is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Southern Super Stars will be considered correct if Southern Super Stars is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Legends Cricket League: 3rd Place vs 4th Place - Most Sixes 3rd Place Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between 3rd Place and 4th Place scheduled for 2026-03-25 in Legends Cricket League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to 3rd Place will be considered correct if 3rd Place is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than 4th Place.The outcome corresponding to 4th Place will be considered correct if 4th Place is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than 3rd Place.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: UCF Knights vs. UCLA Bruins: O/U 151.5
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for March 20 at 7:25 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the UCF Knights and UCLA Bruins combine to score 152 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 152, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: UCLA Bruins hold a 67% implied probability as trader consensus in the LA Bowl matchup against UCF Knights largely due to home-field edge at SoFi Stadium and superior roster talent. UCLA enters 5-7 after a gritty late-season push with wins over Washington and Fresno State, showcasing QB Ethan Garbers' efficiency (over 3,000 yards) against UCF's inconsistent secondary. UCF (6-6) faltered on the road, dropping four of five away games amid turnover woes from freshman QB Cam Fancher. No major injuries reported on official updates—both teams near full strength—but UCLA's pass rush (35 sacks) exploits UCF's offensive line vulnerabilities, tilting odds despite bowl unpredictability.

question: U.S. forces seize another oil tanker by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if U.S. government forces seize an oil tanker or any other ship actively transporting oil between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

U.S. government forces refer to any active U.S. military (including U.S. Coast Guard), law enforcement, or intelligence personnel or contractors.

Seizes refers to U.S. forces taking custody of or asserting operational control of the vessel, including boarding and taking control of the vessel, detaining the vessel indefinitely, or forcefully rerouting the vessel to a U.S.-controlled port.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors "No" at 81% for U.S. forces seizing another oil tanker by March 31, driven by the absence of fresh intelligence, official announcements, or operational signals following the January 16 seizure of the M/T SANI near the Arabian Peninsula for alleged Iranian sanctions violations. U.S. Navy focus has shifted to defending commercial shipping against ongoing Houthi attacks in the Red Sea, with recent airstrikes targeting Houthi sites rather than tanker interdictions. No Treasury sanctions or CENTCOM statements indicate imminent actions, and the tight timeline reduces likelihood amid de-escalation talks and stabilized tanker routes.

question: US forces enter Iran by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices near-zero odds on US ground forces entering Iran by year-end, reflecting the Biden administration's strict avoidance of direct military confrontation amid Israel's recent airstrikes on Iranian targets and Tehran's missile retaliation. question: Houthi military action against Israel by April 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Houthis initiate a drone, missile, or air strike that impacts Israeli ground territory between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Houthi forces that physically impact land under Israeli control.

Israeli ground territory, for the purposes of this market, refers to land internationally recognized as part of the State of Israel, including all territory under de facto Israeli government administration as of market creation. This includes the Israeli-controlled Golan Heights and East Jerusalem, but excludes the West Bank and Gaza Strip.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted before reaching the ground, or surface-to-air missile strikes, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution—regardless of whether debris lands on Israeli soil or causes damage.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Houthi drone and missile launches targeting Israel, including a July strike on Tel Aviv and coordinated fire during Iran's October 1 barrage—all intercepted except the prior hit—drive trader consensus on potential escalation. The Iran-backed rebels from Yemen frame attacks as solidarity with Gaza and Lebanon amid ongoing Israel-Hamas and Hezbollah conflicts, prompting Israeli airstrikes on Houthi ports and leadership. Successful IDF intercepts have limited damage, but degraded Red Sea shipping and U.S.-led coalition strikes weaken Houthi capabilities. Traders monitor Israel's vowed responses to Iran and any Houthi retaliation, with U.S. election dynamics possibly influencing naval support levels.

question: Will another country conduct military action against Iran by April 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any country other than Israel or the United States initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying “military action” is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by a country other than Israel or the United States’ military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile or drone launched by such a country, this market will resolve to “Yes”).

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground forces of countries other than Israel or the United States will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

question: Maren Haile-Selassie: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming MLS game between Philadelphia Union and Chicago Fire FC, scheduled for March 21, 2026 at 4:30 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Maren Haile-Selassie is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Will either Tate brother be arrested by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if either Andrew Tate or Tristan Tate is arrested or detained by law enforcement by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if either Andrew Tate or Tristan Tate is arrested or detained by law enforcement by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts against an imminent arrest for either Andrew or Tristan Tate, with implied probabilities hovering below 20%, primarily due to their ongoing judicial controls in Romania allowing supervised EU travel since August 2024, following a court ruling easing prior house arrest restrictions. Recent developments, including Romania's rejection of their appeal against a UK extradition warrant in late September, have fueled speculation but no immediate action, as prosecutors prioritize the core human trafficking case expected to drag into 2025. US interest persists via FBI probes, yet lacks formal extradition moves; watch for DIICOT updates or UK hearings as key catalysts that could spike odds amid the brothers' vocal online defiance amplifying cultural polarization.

question: Will Israel launch a major ground offensive in Gaza by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a major ground offensive in Gaza by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "major ground offensive" is defined as a large-scale military operation that involves more than 1,000 Israeli ground forces entering Gazan territory that was not under Israeli control at the start of the offensive. This excludes smaller raids, special operations, or limited incursions directly bordering buffer zones already under Israeli control.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Israel launch a major ground offensive in Gaza by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a major ground offensive in Gaza between May 5, 9:00 PM ET, and May 13, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "major ground offensive" is defined as a large-scale military operation that involves more than 1,000 Israeli ground forces entering Gazan territory that was not under Israeli control at the start of the offensive. This excludes smaller raids, special operations, or limited incursions directly bordering buffer zones already under Israeli control.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israel's targeted raids and airstrikes in northern Gaza, including Jabalia and Beit Hanoun, have intensified amid ongoing hostage talks mediated by the US, Qatar, and Egypt, but fall short of a major ground offensive involving large-scale troop deployments like the initial post-October 7 incursion. Prime Minister Netanyahu's insistence on resuming full operations if negotiations fail drives trader caution, tempered by US warnings against Rafah escalation and Biden administration aid conditions. Recent IDF successes against Hamas tunnels and commanders bolster Israel's position without broad invasion, while Hamas rocket fire sustains pressure. Traders watch Doha talks and a potential UN Security Council vote this week, which could shift escalation risks and market odds.

question: Spread: Real Valladolid CF (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for March 31 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Real Valladolid CF" if Real Valladolid CF win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Cádiz CF".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Real Valladolid CF vs. Cádiz CF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for March 31 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Valladolid CF holds a slight edge in trader consensus for their home clash against Cádiz CF in La Liga 2, driven by superior recent form with three wins in their last five matches compared to Cádiz's lone victory amid a four-game winless streak. Key development: Valladolid striker Mamadou Sylla is fully fit after a minor knock, bolstering their attack that averages 1.8 goals per home game, while Cádiz midfielder Chris Ramos remains sidelined with a hamstring strain per official reports. Home advantage shines historically—Valladolid unbeaten in last three vs. Cádiz—though Cádiz's desperation for points in the relegation scrap adds upset risk; watch weather and ref assignments for final tweaks.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
